LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ZIMMER, INC. AND ZIMMER, FEGAN & MALONEY, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PARTICULAR ALLEGATIONS OF PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF NOW HAS FACTS WHICH SHE CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH IN SUPPORT OF A PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIM, OR WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF'S RIGHTS AT ANY TIME TO SEEK LEAVE TO REQUEST PUNITIVE DAMAGES IF SHE AT SOME POINT OBTAINS FACTS SUPPORTING THE REQUEST. THE "EXEMPLARY DAMAGE CLAIM" ALLEGED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS, AS OPPOSED TO LEGAL CONCLUSIONS, ESTABLISHING PLAINTIFF'S ENTITLEMENT TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. TIMOTHY G. TIETJEN, ESQ. of Rouda Feder Tietjen McGuinn, Tel No. 415-398-5398 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. SONJA S. WEISSMAN, ESQ. of Reed Smith LLP, Tel No. 415-659-4874 appearing for the Defendant Zimmer, Inc. and Zimmer and Fegan & Maloney, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court modifies the language of its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: As to DEFENDANTS ZIMMER, INC. AND ZIMMER, FEGAN & MALONEY, INC'S Motion To Strike Particular Allegations Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint, Defendants' motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the first amended complaint is granted with 30 days leave to amend if Plaintiff now has facts which she can allege in good faith in support of a punitive damage claim, or without prejudice to plaintiff's rights at any time to seek leave to request punitive damages if she at some point obtains facts supporting the request. The "exemplary damage claim" alleged in the first amended complaint does not allege ultimate facts, as opposed to legal conclusions, establishing plaintiff's entitlement to punitive damages. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. TIMOTHY G. TIETJEN, ESQ. of Rouda Feder Tietjen McGuinn, Tel No. 415-398-5398 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. SONJA S. WEISSMAN, ESQ. of Reed Smith LLP, Tel No. 415-659-4874 appearing for the Defendant Zimmer, Inc. and Zimmer and Fegan & Maloney, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a single modification as to number of days leave to amend. As to DEFENDANTS ZIMMER, INC. AND ZIMMER AND FEGAN & MALONEY, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of express warranty in the first amended complaint is sustained with 30 leave to amend. The express warranty claim fails to allege the specific source of the warranty and fails to attach the source document(s) or quote the warranty language therein. The current allegations about the warranty are too general and conclusory. Plaintiff is given to leave to correct these deficiencies if she can do so in good faith. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
